description: Tests whether retrieval surfaces evidence from services not mentioned in the query. Query mentions 'checkout' (cart-svc) but root cause is in inventory-svc. Also tests multi-service correlation and causal chain discovery.

questions.question: why did checkout start failing
questions.ground_truth_answer: Migration 0042_add_warehouse_id.sql on inventory-svc added a NOT NULL warehouse_id column, causing all SKU inserts to fail with 'null value in column warehouse_id violates not-null constraint'. Cart-svc retried those 500 responses until its HTTP client pool to inventory-svc was EXHAUSTED (50/50 in use), leading to checkout timeouts.

questions.question: what caused the cart timeouts
questions.ground_truth_answer: Cart-svc timeouts occurred because the HTTP client pool to inventory-svc was EXHAUSTED (50/50 in use). Inventory-svc was returning 500 errors due to migration 0042 that added a NOT NULL warehouse_id column, triggering a retry storm with 22 in-flight retries that consumed all connection pool slots.

questions.question: inventory-svc 500 errors
questions.ground_truth_answer: Inventory-svc returned 500 errors because migration 0042_add_warehouse_id.sql added a NOT NULL warehouse_id column to the skus table without a default value. All subsequent SKU insert operations failed with 'null value in column warehouse_id violates not-null constraint'.

questions.question: what was the impact of the inventory-svc database migration
questions.ground_truth_answer: Migration 0042_add_warehouse_id.sql caused a cascading failure. Inventory-svc SKU inserts failed with not-null constraint violations returning 500 errors. Cart-svc retried aggressively (retry storm: 22 in-flight retries), exhausting its HTTP client pool (50/50 in use). This caused cart operation timeouts and checkout failures for multiple cart IDs.

questions.question: how did the retry storm start
questions.ground_truth_answer: Cart-svc's upstream calls to inventory-svc started getting 500 responses after migration 0042 broke SKU inserts. Cart-svc retried each failed request up to 5 times, and with multiple concurrent requests all retrying, it created a retry storm of 22 in-flight retries that exhausted the HTTP client pool (50/50 in use).

questions.question: connection pool exhaustion in the cart service
questions.ground_truth_answer: Cart-svc's HTTP client pool to inventory-svc became EXHAUSTED with 50/50 connections in use. This was caused by inventory-svc returning 500 errors after migration 0042, which triggered aggressive retries. New requests queued waiting for inventory client slots, leading to cart operation timeouts and checkout failures.
